incorrects.description: Student selected 8, represents whole-number addition error (3 + 5)

params.description: Products strip highlights 5, 10, 15 to show the sequence.

steps.text: You added 3 and 5. This problem asks you to multiply. Skip-count by 5s: start at 5, then 10, then 15. Place the third number.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, stopped after second skip-count step

params.description: Products strip highlights 5, 10, 15 with emphasis on the third position.

steps.text: 10 is the second multiple of 5. You need 3 groups of 5. Keep going: 5, 10... what comes next?

incorrects.description: Student selected 20, skip-counted four times instead of three

params.description: Products strip highlights 5, 10, 15 to isolate the first three multiples.

steps.text: 20 is the fourth multiple of 5. You only need 3 groups. Count with me: 5, 10, 15. That's three. Place 15.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Products strip highlights sequentially as system counts: 5 (first group), 10 (second group), 15 (third group). 15 tile animates into product slot.

steps.text: Let me show you. 3 times 5 means 3 groups of 5. I skip-count by 5s three times: 5, 10, 15. Three groups gets me to 15. That's the product. The answer is 15.
